title: CatBoost rank-ensemble with raw categoricals and segment specialists
summary: The data has high-cardinality Driver plus interaction categories, and CatBoost’s ordered target statistics are designed to reduce target leakage from categorical encodings.
rationale: The data has high-cardinality Driver plus interaction categories, and CatBoost’s ordered target statistics are designed to reduce target leakage from categorical encodings. A rank-averaged ensemble of CatBoost with the current LightGBM/AutoGluon-style model can add diversity without another large feature expansion.
implementation_hint: Train CatBoostClassifier with raw Driver/Race/Compound and engineered numeric features, using ordered boosting, class weights or scale_pos_weight, AUC eval metric, and GroupKFold by Year/Race. Blend by rank averaging with the current best model; optionally add specialist models for wet compounds, dry compounds, early/mid/late race bins.
expected_effect: Likely modest but robust AUC gain from better categorical handling and model diversity, especially for rare drivers/races and interaction-heavy contexts.
risk: CatBoost may be slower; careless target/frequency encodings alongside CatBoost CTRs can duplicate leakage-prone signals, so compare raw-categorical and engineered-only variants.
